no: SCS should be the LAST tactical mod installed. All creature mods (MiH E&Q, BGSpawn), encounter mods (BuTcHeRy, Tactics Remix, The Crucible), and content mods must install BEFORE SCS. SCS reads the game state and builds AI scripts based on what is installed — installing content after SCS means that content won't get SCS's AI improvements. INSTALL FIX REQUIRED: SFO lib_spl.tph line 1092 has DECOMPILE_AND_PATCH on all BCS files — fails on BDAERIEC.BCS and A7!GXRU.BCS. Patch stratagems/sfo2e/lib_spl.tph to add PATCH_IF NOT (SOURCE_RES = BDAERIEC OR A7!GXRU) guard. Same fix needed in 5 other SFO copies (ToF, IWDification, MiH FR/SP/Metamod). Automated fixes: eet-mod-forge-patches components #4-5.

conflicts.reason: Stratagems will not use revised HLAs
conflicts.theirComps: #10 Install revised HLAs for all classes #11 #101 #102 #103 #104 #105 #106 #107 #108 #109 #110

conflicts.reason: SCS cn:6550 (Smarter Beholders) rewrites beholder AI including antimagic ray behavior. If JTweaks cn:181/182 is installed first, SCS overwrites those changes — JTweaks Antimagic component becomes ineffective. If using SCS Smarter Beholders, do not install JTweaks cn:181/182. JT cn:181/182 are subcomponents (pick one) — cn:181 makes rays ignore Spell Shield, cn:182 lets Spell Shield block one ray.
conflicts.source: SCS rewrites beholder AI; JTweaks antimagic changes overwritten; JT cn:181/182, 2026-04

conflicts.reason: Install order note. SCS Smarter Ascension components (cn:6590/6840/6850) are designed to install AFTER Ascension and supersede the Tougher battle components (cn:8-13) with improved AI. SCS detects Ascension and applies its own tactical scripts on top. Standard EET install order handles this — Ascension installs in QUEST MODS, SCS installs in TACTICAL MODS (later). Not a conflict if install order is correct.
conflicts.myComps: #6590 Smarter final villain #6840 Ascension Irenicus/Sendai #6850 Ascension demons
conflicts.source: SCS detects Ascension via MOD_IS_INSTALLED; standard category order places SCS after Ascension, 2026-04

conflicts.reason: SCS cn:1500/1510/1520 are the same CamDawg/DavidW IWD spell ports as IWDification cn:30/40/50. SCS spell tweaks (cn:2000-2080) modify spells IWDification also adds. SCS has built-in IWDification detection. IWDification cn:30/40/50, SCS cn:1500-1520 (ToF provides IWD spells). No conflict.
conflicts.theirComps: #30 IWD Arcane #40 IWD Divine #50 Bard Songs (none in Ultimate preset)

conflicts.reason: NOT a conflict. SCS cn:4100 reads kit field but never writes during installation. Runtime AddKit() in dialogue. AK assignments persist.

SCS cn:4100 (leveller) and NPC_EE cn:2000 coexist — SCS zeros NPC levels but not inventory, NPC_EE tome persists. SCS cn:4100 has mutual FORBID with ToF cn:60300 — one leveller can install. s with ToF cn:60300, SCS cn:4100 auto-skips. NPC_EE works with whichever leveller is active.
conflicts.myComps: #4100 Improved NPC customization #4115 Thief skill multiples of five
conflicts.source: SCS leveller_2.tpa same system as ToF; mutual FORBID at SCS tp2 line 693 and ToF tp2 line 1285, 2026-04

conflicts.reason: TnB cn:40 (Revised Specialists) — would conflict with SCS spell school changes. TnB cn:51 (Metamagic) adds innate abilities independent of SCS spell tweaks. Install TnB before SCS.

SCS cn:5000 (Ease-of-use Party AI) and EPS cn:15 (Pre-Buffing) both provide player party automation scripts. They can coexist — SCS provides basic party AI (auto-attack, auto-heal), EPS provides pre-combat buffing sequences. EPS scripts are assigned to specific party members and don't override SCS party AI assignments. SCS cn:5900 (Initialize AI) is enemy AI infrastructure — no overlap with EPS player scripts. EPS cn:9 (Celestials) and cn:10 (Simulacrum) enhance summoned creature AI — complementary with SCS enemy AI. Install SCS before EPS so EPS scripts layer on top.
conflicts.source: SCS cn:5000 provides basic party AI; EPS provides pre-buffing scripts; different script slots; cn:5900 is enemy AI init, 2026-04

conflicts.reason: SCS Improved Shade Lord and Shadow Magic's Enhanced Shade Lord both modify the same encounter. Use SM's ability-only option (#8) if using SCS.
SCS has built-in IR detection code. Install IR BEFORE SCS. SCS silently skips overlapping features. Item Revisions and SCS overlap
G-Minion should install before SCS. SCS AI initialization patches over G-Minion's scripts. Install order dependent.

SCS IWD spell import components (#1500/#1510) conflict with IWD-in-EET's own spell resources. Use IWDification for IWD spells instead (canonical G3 source, same DavidW/SFO framework). SCS's AI/tactical components (#5000+) are completely unaffected — install those freely.
conflicts.myComps: #1500 IWD Arcane Spells, #1510 IWD Divine Spells (only these conflict; all AI/tactical components are fine)
conflicts.source: SCS tp2 has REQUIRE_PREDICATE !MOD_IS_INSTALLED iwdification on #1500/#1510; IWDification is the recommended IWD spell source for EET, 2026-03

Install order note. SCS cn:2040/4030 (Improved Shapeshifting) applies AI improvements to shapeshift forms. HT cn:2 replaces druid shapeshifting spells. SCS installs after HT and works on top of whatever forms exist. Install HT before SCS.
conflicts.source: SCS applies AI scripts on top of HT form replacements; standard category order handles this, 2026-04

conflicts.reason: TR v3.0+ is a comprehensive difficulty overhaul that substantially duplicates SCS's AI and encounter systems. User reports treat TR+SCS as effective either-or for the AI/encounter layer; a select few SCS general-game tweaks (non-AI, non-encounter) remain compatible. TR detects SCS and adapts technically, but functional overlap is significant — recommended to pick one or the other. TR's new-encounter components install BEFORE SCS (now encoded via cat override); update-existing-encounters + enemy-improvements install AFTER SCS. [Historical: Broad interaction — TR detects SCS via MOD_IS_INSTALLED for cn:1500, cn:1510, cn:5900, and cn:6030. TR adapts IWD spell handling, conditionally skips its Kangaxx demilich when SCS Smarter Mages is present, and removes SCS troll kill flags from boss weapons. SCS AI/encounter overhauls overlap with TR encounter overhauls — per TR author Morpheus: 'Any encounter or AI updates from SCS will not be compatible with Tactics Remix. SCS QoL updates prior to AI/tactical components should mostly be okay.' SCS must install before TR.]
conflicts.myComps: #1500 IWD Arcane / #1510 IWD Divine / #5900 Initialize AI / #6030 Smarter Mages / #8060 Improved de'Arnise Keep
conflicts.theirComps: #0 Improved de'Arnise Keep / #15 Tougher Kangaxx / #65 #66 Improved Irenicus / all encounter components
conflicts.source: TR TP2 MOD_IS_INSTALLED checks, lib/always.tph scs_ai/scs_smrt_mgs vars, Morpheus compatibility statement, G3 forums April 2026

conflicts.reason: ToF Point-Buy Stats (cn:80100-80104) have FORBID_COMPONENT SCS cn:5900 (Initialise AI). Point-buy auto-skips if SCS is installed. Cannot use both — choose SCS tactical AI or ToF point-buy.

Install-order-dependent: ToF's tp2 has 77× FORBID_COMPONENT ~stratagems~ #5900 — if SCS #5900 (Initialise AI) installs BEFORE ToF, 20+ ToF components (#80150-#90100) silently skip. This is ToF-side enforcement, not SCS-side. Install ToF BEFORE SCS to avoid.
conflicts.theirComps: #20000 Subraces #40000 Class/race restrictions #40100 Alignment #40150 #40160 #40190 Kits #50600 Blackguards #55000 Druid multiclass #60100 Minor abilities #80150 3E THAC0 #80160 #80500 INT spell restrictions #81000 Cleric/Ranger bows #81010 #81020 #81030 Dual-class prof #81100 Weapon Proficiency #81200 Weapon Styles #90000 Class menu #90100 Thief skills
conflicts.source: Audit 2026-04: grep of dw_talents.tp2 found 77× FORBID_COMPONENT stratagems/setup-stratagems.tp2 5900 covering 20 of the user's skipped ToF components

conflicts.reason: Install-order-dependent predicate: cdtweaks #4020 (ToB-Style NPCs) will skip if SCS #4100 (Improved NPC Customisation) is already installed. SCS #4100 supersedes cdtweaks #4020. Intentional by CamDawg via !MOD_IS_INSTALLED predicate.

SCS spell/IWD components (cn:1500-2080) auto-skip if ToF equivalents installed (REQUIRE_PREDICATE checks in tp2). Already handled — SCS cn:1500-1520 (ToF provides IWD spells). SCS cn:4030 (Improved Shapeshifting) and ToF cn:40600 (Druid Shapeshift) both modify druid shapeshifting — SCS applies AI improvements, ToF replaces forms. Install SCS after ToF. SCS cn:4050 (Reputation Rate) has no ToF equivalent — independent. SCS cn:4115/4130/4240 already SCS cn:60300 vs ToF cn:60300 — mutual FORBID, SCS cn:4100 removed.
conflicts.myComps: #4030 Improved Shapeshifting (order note) #4050 Reputation Rate (safe) #1500-#2080 Spell/IWD (auto-skip if ToF present)
conflicts.theirComps: #40600 Druid shapeshift #1500-#2080 Spell equivalents #60300 NPC customisation #90100 Thief skill multiples
conflicts.source: SCS tp2 lines 129-318: REQUIRE_PREDICATE !MOD_IS_INSTALLED dw_talents for spell components; mutual FORBID on cn:4100/4115/4130/4240, 2026-04

conflicts.reason: Design choice, not technical conflict. SCS cn:4145 (Remove tutorial NPCs) is compatible — just removes hand-holding NPCs. SCS cn:4146 (Skip Candlekeep entirely) bypasses the prologue, making Candlekeep Revisited content inaccessible. Do not use cn:4146 if you want Candlekeep Revisited content. No file-level conflict — purely a content accessibility choice.
conflicts.myComps: #4145 Remove tutorial NPCs (compatible) #4146 Skip Candlekeep entirely (defeats purpose)
conflicts.source: cn:4145 removes NPCs only; cn:4146 skips area; content choice not file conflict, 2026-04

Compatible with SCS per user reports. No specific install order requirement.

SCS #4030 (Improved shapeshifting) has !(MOD_IS_INSTALLED ~dw_talents~ 40600) and !(MOD_IS_INSTALLED ~dw_talents~ 41000) predicates. If either ToF component is installed, SCS #4030 silently skips. Knock-on: Bart_Tweaks #2030 (Revised Shapeshifting Tokens for SCS) then also skips because it requires SCS #4030.

ki.description: SPLSTATE.IDS has a 256-entry limit. SCS adds ~40 entries, EE Fixpack ~20, Enhanced Powergaming Scripts ~20. Kit mods that add states can push over the limit.
ki.workaround: Reduce the number of kit mods, especially I Hate Undead, large kitpacks. Check SPLSTATE.IDS in your override folder — if it has >250 entries, you are at risk.

ki.description: SCS v35.21 cn:1510 (IWD Divine) reports namespace conflict on EXALTAH.VVC, and cn:1520 (IWD Bard Songs) reports namespace conflict on OHVRPO.PRO (5 bard song spells) when EE Fixpack Alpha is installed before SCS. Install completes successfully but resources may not be copied.
ki.workaround: These are warnings, not errors. Likely resolved when EE Fixpack Alpha exits alpha. Monitor for gameplay impact on Exaltation spell and bard song projectiles.

ki.pattern: ranged but not melee|has ranged but not melee weapon
ki.description: SCS cn:6000 (Initialize AI) warns about ~20 creatures that have ranged but no melee weapons (C6WULF, HABIB, QUAYLE, VAELAG, WULFGAR, etc.). Also reports failed script insert: dw#gphlp above gpmerc in OHRSGUA1. These are informational warnings during SCS AI initialization.
ki.workaround: No action needed — SCS handles these gracefully. Creatures will use ranged weapons only.

ki.description: Lua error 'string M_DW_OOD.lua:301: unexpected symbol' reported after SCS install. Suspected Infinity UI compatibility issue. Single report (shadow85, March 2026), unconfirmed.
ki.workaround: If using Infinity UI, set Force_Difficulty_Widget=1 in stratagems.ini and avoid SCS cn:4115 (Thieves skill points in multiples of five). If error persists, try without Infinity UI.

ki.description: Late-sequence SCS batches can cascade into slowdowns lasting tens of minutes to over an hour per batch. SCS shares the SFO library with Talents of Faerun, IWDification, and MiH mods — all accumulate SFO memory pressure. Observed: session 88733aac (2026-04-16) batch 450 ran 15min, batch 451 ran 1.1h with throughput cascade detected at 30min (417 → 147 lines/min). The slowest SCS component is typically cn:6000 (Initialise AI) which processes every creature file in the game with heavy ALTER_SCRIPT_BLOCK and COPY_EXISTING loops. Previous sessions (3e3996c9, c623ff72) also showed SCS batches running 3-9min each in the 440-455 range.
ki.workaround: If an SCS batch exceeds ~10 minutes, the slow-batch watchdog will warn in gui.log with the last WeiDU output line. The batch is not stuck — it's working but slow. Aborting and restarting usually resumes past the slow batch (checkpoint recovery). For faster installs, consider reducing SCS component count — cn:6000 (Initialise AI) is the single longest component and can be deferred to a lighter install.
ki.source: Session references extracted from description on 2026-04-21 during Phase 2.3 validator rollout: session 88733aac

ki.pattern: finbalth\.bcs.*PARSE ERROR|Smarter Throne of Bhaal final villain.*NOT INSTALLED|Smarter Illasera.*NOT INSTALLED|Smarter Gromnir.*NOT INSTALLED
ki.description: SCS ToB final-villain components (cn:6590 Smarter Throne of Bhaal final villain, cn:6840 Smarter Illasera, cn:6860 Smarter Gromnir) call DECOMPILE_AND_PATCH on finbalth.bcs — a 44 KB override script with dense AI blocks. The round-trip produces BAF that fails to re-parse ('PARSE ERROR at line 124 column 1-40, Near Text: )'), likely because SCS's disjunctive_substitution output has unbalanced parentheses in an OR() block for this specific script's trigger shape. Observed in session 6693001a (2026-04-16): all 3 components rolled back, cascading into 48 downstream SCS batches (455-470) silently skipping their dependents over ~45 seconds due to failed REQUIRE_COMPONENT predicates.
ki.workaround: The DECOMPILE_AND_PATCH failure is at the WeiDU engine level, not inside SFO ALTER_SCRIPT_BLOCK, so the per-mod PATCH_TRY patches don't cover it. Two mitigations: (1) Enable Infinity Mod Runner's experimental Resilient WeiDU (Setup → Experimental) which catches round-trip failures at the engine level and returns original buffer unchanged; (2) If only a few components are affected, let them fail — the cascade-skipped components are pure tactical improvements and the install is functionally complete without them. Investigating the specific BAF output at line 124 of finbalth.bcs would need SCS-side debugging.
ki.source: Session references extracted from description on 2026-04-21 during Phase 2.3 validator rollout: session 6693001a

ki.pattern: SKIPPING.*Revised Shapeshifting Tokens for SCS|Improved Shapeshifting.*must be installed
ki.description: Bart_Tweaks cn:2030 (Revised Shapeshifting Tokens for SCS) silently skips if SCS #4030 (Improved Shapeshifting) is not installed. SCS #4030 itself auto-skips if ToF #40600 (Revised Druid Shapeshifting) or #41000 (Rebalanced Kits) is installed. 3-way chain: ToF #40600/#41000 installed → SCS #4030 skips → Bart #2030 skips.
ki.workaround: Either: (A) Skip ToF #40600/#41000 and enable SCS #4030 + Bart #2030; OR (B) Keep ToF and drop SCS #4030 + Bart #2030 from your preset. Cannot use all three together.

sum: SCS pre-tactical section: AI initialization, spell tweaks, and setup components that must install before the main tactical changes.

rec.tip: Install on Core/Hard first. Insane SCS is a very different game. Use Enhanced Powergaming Scripts for auto-buffing to match.

Overhauls NPC customization: proficiencies, spells, kits. POST EET component. FORBID cdtweaks cn:2370/2372 and ToF cn:60300.

co.n: Thieves assign skill points in multiples of five
co.no: Don't mix with SubtleD's NPC EE or ToF's equivalent
co.tp2n: Thieves assign skill points in multiples of five

co.n: Revised handling of death effects like disintegration, petrification and imprisonment (party members who are disintegrated etc can be resurrected; imprisoned or petriied characters rejoin the party
co.no: Use this only if not installing ToF's equivalent
co.tp2n: Revised handling of death effects like disintegration, petrification and imprisonment (party members who are disintegrated etc can be resurrected; imprisoned or petrified characters rejoin the party automatically; the game doesn't end if the main character is petrified or imprisoned)

co.n: Revised resting: resting in the wild uses up provisions
co.no: Resting in the wild consumes provisions from inventory. Adds resource management.
co.tp2n: Revised resting: resting in the wild uses up provisions

co.n: Revised inn rooms: more expensive, more benefits
co.no: Inn rooms cost more but provide better healing and spell restoration benefits.
co.tp2n: Revised inn rooms: more expensive, more benefits

co.no: Convenient but second option defeats purpose of Candlekeep Revisited
co.scn: Skip the Candlekeep tutorial sections
co.tp2n: Remove the tutorial NPCs from Candlekeep

co.scn: Skip the Candlekeep tutorial sections
co.no: Skips Candlekeep tutorial entirely and starts in the wilderness. Breaks fourth wall.
co.tp2n: Skip Candlekeep altogether (warning: breaks the 4th wall!)

co.n: Allow the Cowled Wizards to detect spellcasting in most indoor, above-ground areas in Athkatla
co.tp2n: Allow the Cowled Wizards to detect spellcasting in most indoor, above-ground areas in Athkatla

co.no: v Magic license price
co.scn: Increase the price of a license to practise magic in Athkatla
Increase the price of a license to practise magic in Athkatla
Increase the price of a license to practise magic in Athkatla
Increase the price of a license to practise magic in Athkatla
Increase the price of a license to practise magic in Athkatla

co.no: Moot when I4E or Imoen Romance is installed

Increase the price asked by Gaelan Bayle
Increase the price asked by Gaelan Bayle
Increase the price asked by Gaelan Bayle
Increase the price asked by Gaelan Bayle

co.n: Make Watchers' Keep accessible between SoA and ToB (WARNING: this will make it inaccessible until the end of SoA)
co.tp2n: Make Watchers' Keep accessible between SoA and ToB (warning: this will make it inaccessible until the end of SoA)
co.no: Makes Watcher's Keep accessible between SoA and ToB instead of only during one campaign.

co.n: Randomize the maze in Watcher's Keep
co.no: v35.19 has chapter check issue. Fixed in v35.20+
co.tp2n: Randomize the maze in Watcher's Keep

co.n: Remove unrealistically helpful items from certain areas
co.no: Removes unrealistically placed powerful items from certain areas (e.g., scrolls in easy dungeons).
co.tp2n: Remove unrealistically helpful items from certain areas

co.no: v Convenient ammo removal
co.scn: Remove unrealistically convenient ammunition from the game
co.tp2n: Only remove nonmagical ammo from random containers

co.scn: Remove unrealistically convenient ammunition from the game
co.no: Ammo removal variant: removes up to +2 enchanted ammo from containers.
co.tp2n: Remove ammo up to the +2 level from random containers

co.scn: Remove unrealistically convenient ammunition from the game
co.no: Ammo removal variant: removes all ammo from containers.
co.tp2n: Remove all ammo from random containers

co.n: Delay the arrival of the "bonus merchants" in the Adventurers' Mart and the Copper Coronet
co.no: Delays bonus merchants in Adventurer's Mart and Copper Coronet until later chapters.
co.tp2n: Delay the arrival of the "bonus merchants" in the Adventurers' Mart and the Copper Coronet

co.n: Treat mages' and priests' High-Level Abilities as innate abilities rather than memorisable spells (each may be taken only once)
co.no: Treats mage/priest HLAs as innate abilities (each taken once). Reads existing HLA tables — install AFTER all HLA table mods (Refinements, ToF, RR). FORBID Spell Revisions.
co.tp2n: Treat mages' and priests' High-Level Abilities as innate abilities rather than memorisable spells (each may be taken only once)

co.n: Make spell sequencers and contingencies into innate abilities
co.no: Blocked by Tome and Blood metamagic system (qdtnb_metamagic.qd).
co.tp2n: Make spell sequencers and contingencies into innate abilities

co.no: Replaces standard Advanced AI with SCS party scripts. Auto-heals out of combat, intelligent target selection in combat, hotkey-based defensive prebuffing (C=Chaotic Commands, V=Death Ward, etc.). Precasting disabled by default for sorcerers/shamans. Complements EPS pre-buffing scripts (install SCS first).

co.n: Cosmetic change: stop Stoneskins from changing the caster's colour
co.no: Original BG2:ToB engine only. Not compatible with Enhanced Edition.
co.tp2n: Cosmetic change: stop Stoneskins from changing the caster's colour

co.no: Adds flavor text screens between BG1 chapters, similar to BG2's chapter narrations.

co.n: Initialise AI components (required for all tactical and AI components)
co.no: REQUIRED for all SCS tactical and AI components (cn:6000-8190). Initializes AI framework.
co.tp2n: Initialise AI components (required for all tactical and AI components)

co.no: Core combat AI: enemies use better tactics, positioning, and target selection.
Enemies call for reinforcements from nearby areas.

co.n: IWD: Smarter general AI and better calls for help
co.tp2n: Smarter general AI and better calls for help
co.no: IWDEE-only. Requires SCS #5900 (Initialise AI) installed.
Limit on help calls means encounter groups designed to work independently
Priest enemies cast prebuffs, use defensive spells, and heal intelligently.
Enemy NPCs use healing and buff potions in combat.
Spiders use web and poison more tactically. BG1 areas.
Sirines use charm and abilities more effectively. BG1 areas.
Carrion crawlers use paralysis attacks more intelligently.
Basilisks use petrification gaze more tactically. BG1 areas.
.ini No_Cursed_Golem_Wounds=1 to skip cursed fist on clay golems
Personal note: .ini No_Cursed_Golem_Wounds set to 1 to skip the cursed fist on clay golems
Some current problems with summoned creature AI
Dragons use full spell repertoire and better combat tactics.
Beholders use antimagic rays and eye stalks strategically.
Mind flayers use psionic blast and devour brain more effectively.
Githyanki use their psionic abilities and silver swords.
Vampires use domination, level drain, and gaseous form tactically.

co.n: Smarter Throne of Bhaal final villain
co.no: Amelyssan uses improved AI and full spell repertoire.
co.tp2n: Smarter Throne of Bhaal final villain

co.no: IWDEE-only. Requires SCS #5900 (Initialise AI) installed.
Illasera uses better tactics and positioning.
Gromnir uses improved combat AI.
Yaga-Shura uses fire giant abilities and better tactics.
Abazigal uses dragon abilities more effectively.

co.n: Ascension versions of Irenicus and Sendai use SCS abilities and AI
co.no: Ascension Irenicus and Sendai use SCS-enhanced AI.
co.tp2n: Ascension versions of Irenicus and Sendai use SCS abilities and AI

co.n: Ascension demons use SCS abilities and AI
co.no: Ascension demons use SCS fiend AI and abilities.
co.tp2n: Ascension demons use SCS abilities and AI

co.no: Doppelgangers use better disguise and ambush tactics.

co.n: Tougher Black Talons and Iron Throne guards
co.no: Black Talon and Iron Throne mercenaries are better equipped and use tactics.
co.tp2n: Tougher Black Talons and Iron Throne guards

co.n: Improved deployment for parties of assassins
co.no: Assassin parties use better positioning and ambush deployment.
co.tp2n: Improved deployment for parties of assassins

co.no: Kobolds use fire arrows, traps, and ambush tactics more effectively.
Bounty hunters appear at different locations for variety.
Ulcaster dungeon enemies use better tactics.
Balduran's Isle encounters are more challenging with better tactics.
Durlag's Tower encounters use improved AI throughout.
Demon cultists use summoning and dark magic more effectively.
Cloakwood druids use nature spells and shapeshifting.
Bassilus uses undead minions and necromancy more effectively.
Drasus party members use coordinated tactics.
Red Wizards use high-level evocation and conjuration.
Undercity assassins use backstab and poison tactics.
Carsa/Kahrk interaction improved with better dialogue and combat.
Chapter 2 end battle (Nashkel Mines) is more challenging.
Chapter 3 end battle is more challenging.
Chapter 4 end battle is more challenging.
Chapter 5 end battle is more challenging.
Final battle with Sarevok is significantly more challenging.
Various minor BG1 encounters use improved AI.

co.n: Make the starting dungeon slightly harder
co.no: Irenicus dungeon encounters are slightly harder.
co.tp2n: Make the starting dungeon slightly harder

co.no: Incompatible with Shadow Magic Enhanced Shade Lord. Choose one
Unusual that demiliches don't cast in vanilla

co.n: Tie difficulty of level-dependent monster groupings to the difficulty slider
co.tp2n: Tie difficulty of level-dependent monster groupings to the difficulty slider
co.no: As hard as this make them, it is weird they don't cast in vanilla
Random encounters on world map are more varied and challenging.
de'Arnise Keep uses Tactics Remix-style encounter improvements.
.ini No_Spirit_Troll_Magic=1 to remove spirit trolls
Bodhi uses improved vampire tactics and abilities.

co.n: Ascension version of Bodhi uses Improved-Bodhi abilities
co.no: Ascension Bodhi uses SCS Improved Bodhi abilities.
co.tp2n: Ascension version of Bodhi uses Improved-Bodhi abilities

co.n: Party's items are taken from them in Spellhold
co.no: .ini Spellhold_Easycore=1 to get gear back before Irenicus fight
co.tp2n: Party's items are taken from them in Spellhold

co.n: Improved battle with Irenicus in Spellhold
co.no: Ask yourself, do you really have the time for the extra inventory management? Still, you can set Spellhold_Easycore in the .ini to 1 to get the gear back before the Irenicus fight
co.tp2n: Improved battle with Irenicus in Spellhold

co.no: Sahuagin City encounters use better tactics.
Beholder hive uses coordinated antimagic and eye stalk attacks.
Trolls regenerate consistently and require fire or acid to kill permanently. Install after creature mods.
Caution in Ust Natha encounters
Tougher encounters throughout all levels of Watchers Keep. Significantly increased difficulty.
Fire giant temple encounters use improved tactics.
.ini Sendai_Component_Is_Sendai_Only=1 to change only Sendai
Personal note: .ini Sendai_Component_Is_Sendai_Only set to 1 to change only Sendai. Upgrading the common drow sounds great, but readng the details on the extra magical monsters makes me lazy
Various minor BG2 encounters use improved AI.

advisories.reason: SCS Smarter Mind Flayers and Rebalanced Troll Regeneration may overwrite Polytweak's creature stat changes. Install Polytweak before SCS.
advisories.myComps: #8060 Smarter mind flayers #8070 Rebalanced troll regeneration

advisories.reason: SCS Smarter Mages may override mage encounter scripts. Install Questpack before SCS.
SCS AI expects vanilla Monk class for enemy monks. MO monks as Thief kits may confuse SCS creature scripts.
ToBR has no SCS detection. Script REPLACE_TEXTUALLY may fail if SCS rewrites GROMNIR.BCS/YAGA01.bcs first. Install ToBR before SCS.
Both overhaul demilich behavior. May produce unexpected interactions
SCS NPC customization may override s9 kit assignments
Creature revision components may overlap with SCS creature improvements. Install MiH before SCS.
advisories.myComps: #1500 #1510 IWD spells #2000 #2010 #2040 #2050 #2060 Spell tweaks #2520 Revised elementals
advisories.theirComps: #0 Revised Corporal Undead, #1 Revised Dragons, #2 Revised Golems, #3 Revised Liches, #4 Revised Lycanthropes, #5 Revised Magical Beasts, #6 Revised Monstrous Spiders, #7 Revised Oozes, #8 Revised Vampires

advisories.reason: Both modify kobold creatures. May conflict depending on install order.

Install order note. HA cn:9 uses EXTEND_TOP on SPSEQ12A.bcs to give scroll to yssold14. SCS cn:6030 (Smarter Mages) may rewrite SPSEQ12A.bcs as part of AI overhaul. Scroll may not auto-spawn but quest still completable via dialogue. Install HA before SCS.
SCS spell changes may interact with FnP sphere system. Compatible with correct install order (FnP before SCS). FnP provides ToF sphere data.
advisories.theirComps: #80 Sphere System #31 Cleric Kits #33 Druid Kits #35 #36 Paladin Kits
